today_in_history_posts.title: p2p authentication and encryption BIPs
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The author has submitted two BIP drafts available on Github, updating cipher suite preferences.
- Changes include adopting Chacha20-Poly1305, deriving keys from the ECDH secret, and introducing a new re-keying method.
- The updated encrypted message format eliminates the SHA256 checksum, aiming for improved performance and invites feedback.

today_in_history_posts.title: Laundry list of inter-peer wire protocol changes
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Reserving before locking in payment channels optimizes to reduce the risk of locking funds.
- The "reserving" stage is key for bi-directional routing and adjusting HTLC time-outs.
- A mix of source-routing and non-source-routing improves privacy and network accessibility.

today_in_history_posts.title: Thoughts on scaling and consensus changes (2023)
today_in_history_posts.summary: - Bitcoin scalability aims for 50,000 off-chain entities to support 1 billion weekly users.  - Increasing block size risks centralization; innovations like Utreexo optimize but don't solve scaling.  - Effective Layer 2 protocols and market pressures are vital for regulatory compliance and security.
